revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:秩父神社御田植祭]]
'''Chichibu Shrine Rice Planting Festival''' (秩父神社御田植祭) is a {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Rice planting festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice planting festival|qq=}} held at {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|en|Chichibu Shrine|qid=Q2963366|lt=Chichibu Shrine|1=Chichibu Shrine}} in Banba-chō, {{ill|Chichibu, Saitama|en|Chichibu, Saitama|qid=Q647079|lt=Chichibu City|1=Chichibu, Saitama}}, Saitama Prefecture. == Overview == Records indicate that the festival began around {{ill|Manji (era)|en|Manji (era)|qid=Q1145295|lt=Manji|1=Manji (era)}} 2 (1659), and it is still conducted in its traditional form today. The shrine grounds are symbolically treated as a {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|神田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred rice field|qq=}}, where participants perform rice planting rituals while singing traditional rice planting songs to pray for a bountiful harvest. Currently, the festival starts at 1:00 PM on {{ill|April 4|en|April 4|qid=Q2508|lt=April 4|1=April 4}}. After a ceremonial service at the {{ill|Honden|en|Honden|qid=Q2044994|lt=main hall|1=Honden}}, the {{ill|Shinko-shiki|lt=Shinko-shiki|lt=Goshinkō|ja|御神幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshinkō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神幸祭}} procession departs for {{ill|Imamiya Shrine|ja|秩父今宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imamiya Shrine|qq=}}, located in Nakachō, Chichibu City. At Imamiya Shrine, a water-fetching ritual is conducted. The procession then returns to Chichibu Shrine, where a water ritual is performed using {{ill|Straw|en|Straw|qid=Q160066|lt=straw|1=Straw}}-crafted {{ill|dragon deity|ja|竜神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dragon deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 龍神|1=Dragon god|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1206058}} at the torii gate. Afterward, a ceremonial meal is presented at the worship hall, followed by a performance of rice cultivation processes by shrine attendants dressed in white garments. They re-enact tasks from "seedbed preparation" to "main field preparation," singing rice planting songs and performing planting actions. The festival concludes with the distribution of ritual offerings, including {{ill|Mochi|en|Mochi|qid=Q470519|lt=mochi|1=Mochi}}, to attendees. On {{ill|March 17|en|March 17|qid=Q2419|lt=March 17, 2009|1=March 17}}, it was designated as an {{ill|intangible folk cultural property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/intangible folk cultural property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24895900}} by Saitama Prefecture.<ref>[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html "Saitama Prefecture List of National and Prefectural Cultural Properties"]</ref> == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Further Reading == *{{Cite journal|和書|author=Seiichirō Asami|title=The Rice Planting Festival at Chichibu Shrine|pages=2-10|journal=Chichibu Folklore|issue=3|publisher=Chichibu Folklore Research Association|year=1969|ncid=AN00142090|ref=harv|qq=}}{{全国書誌番号|00014805|qq=}} *{{Cite journal|和書|author=Hideo Akisawa|title=The Rice Planting Festival at Chichibu Shrine|pages=14-15|journal=Oku Musashi|issue=170|publisher=Oku Musashi Research Association|year=1976|ncid=AN00033765|ref=harv|qq=}}{{全国書誌番号|00002849|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.chichibu-jinja.or.jp/saiten/04_06.htm Rice Planting Festival] - Chichibu Shrine Official Website * [http://www.city.chichibu.lg.jp/menu4347.html Chichibu Shrine Rice Planting Festival] - Chichibu City Official Website {{Event-stub|qq=}} {{Cultural Properties of Japan|qq=}} {{Normdaten|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|秩父神社御田植祭|version=98833511|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:秩父神社御田植祭]]
